title: OpenClaw OpenShell FS bridge write was TOCTOU-racy, letting a symlink swap escape the sandbox mount root
description: OpenShellFsBridge.writeFile() in extensions/openshell/src/fs-bridge.ts (lines 61-92) validates the destination with assertLocalPathSafety() and then, in a separate step, writes by path: it mkdirs parentDir, writes a temp file, and fsPromises.rename(tempPath, hostPath). Because the boundary check and the filesystem mutation operate on path strings at different times (check by path, then mkdir/write/rename by path), an attacker who swaps a parent directory or the leaf for a symlink between the assertLocalPathSafety check and the rename can redirect the write to a location outside target.mountHostRoot. This is a time-of-check/time-of-use (CWE-367) race that breaks the sandbox mount-root pinning for writes.
